accuracy and appropriate scholarly tone How can I maintain manuscript quality when using AI? Strong manuscripts demonstrate clear purpose, methods, and claims, logical reasoning, and authentic scholarly voice. When using AI for drafting assistance, focus on maintaining these quality indicators regardless of what technology you use to support your manuscript preparation. While editorial standards may vary across journals, these basic expectations for integrity and communication can help you evaluate your AI use: Writing to maintain research integrity AI Technologies can sometimes generate plausible-sounding content that lacks depth, create convincing but nonexistent references, or provide generic terminology that doesn't reflect field-specific expertise. To maintain research integrity when using AI: Provide original analysis and an informed critical perspective rather than superficial discussion Support all claims with appropriate evidence and ensure all referenced literature is real and relevant Interpret statistical results accurately and thoroughly, verifying any AI-generated analysis or interpretation Use precise, field-appropriate research terminology Writing to reach your audience AI may produce text with verbose explanations that are unnecessarily complex or provide suggested text that does not match the overall tone of your document. To maintain clear communication and authentic scholarly voice when using AI: Ensure your text makes logical connections between ideas, methods, and conclusions Provide a strong narrative structure that logically guides readers through your findings Keep explanations focused rather than unnecessarily wordy or repetitive Write in a consistent tone that reflects genuine academic engagement with your subject Data visualization, illustrations, and photographs with AI Wiley supports the purposeful use of AI for creating data visualizations and illustrations. While AI can help you create compelling visual content, important restrictions apply to photograph enhancement to protect research integrity. Wiley also provides the following general Guidelines for the preparation of figures to support authors in the development and submission of all figures associated with their manuscript. Can I use AI to support the creation of data visualizations used in my manuscript submission? Yes. Wiley supports the use of AI for creating data visualizations. Data visualizations include charts, graphs, plots, heat maps, scatter plots, statistical analyses, and any other images that display your numerical research data or measurements.. When using AI for data visualizations, you are required to disclose your AI use and verify the accuracy of your submitted image as outlined in the Disclosure and declaration of AI use Author FAQ. Choosing the right AI Technologies for data visualization When working with complex data visualizations, established data visualization software with AI enabled features or specialized data visualization AI Technologies are more effective than general-purpose large language models. General-purpose language models aren't designed for the precision required in data representation, while specialized data visualization Technologies provide more reliable accuracy. Ensuring accuracy and quality Regardless of which Technologies you use, it is your responsibility to verify that your data representations, statistical interpretations, and visual elements accurately reflect your research. For complex visualizations, consider expert review from a co-author or colleague. All figures should meet publication quality standards including appropriate resolution, readable text size, clear legends, and professional formatting. Can I use AI to support the creation of illustrations used in my manuscript submission? Yes. Wiley supports the use of AI for creating research figures and illustrations. Research figures and illustrations include visual representations of concepts, processes, or systems without displaying your actual research data - such as process flows, workflow diagrams, conceptual frameworks, experimental setup illustrations, and flowcharts. When using AI to generate or edit research figures and illustrations, you are required to disclose your AI use and verify the accuracy of your submitted image as outlined in the Disclosure and declaration of AI use Author FAQ . For generated illustration content, ensure compliance with any licensing requirements as outlined in the Content rights and permissions author FAQ. Choosing the right AI Technologies for illustrations For simple diagrams and conceptual illustrations, general-purpose AI illustration generation Technologies can be effective. However, for complex realistic systems, specialized AI illustration tools designed for scientific content provide more accurate results than general-purpose Technologies. Ensuring accuracy and quality You must verify that AI-generated diagrams accurately represent established knowledge and contain no factual errors.. All figures should meet publication quality standards including appropriate resolution, readable text size, clear legends, and professional formatting. Understanding licensing and permissions AI-generated illustrations can incorporate visual elements from the AI's training data, potentially creating licensing restrictions that don't apply to visualizations of your own research data. AI-generated imagery may be subject to usage restrictions, attribution requirements, or commercial limitations that could affect your publication rights. Always check the terms of use for any AI Technologies used, as even "free" Technologies often have licensing restrictions. Can I use AI to enhance photographs in my manuscript? No. Photographs in research manuscripts serve as evidence and artifacts of your research process. AI enhancement tools can introduce unintended alterations that compromise the reliability and reproducibility of your research evidence. Even when AI improvements appear beneficial, they may alter critical details that other researchers need to evaluate your methods and findings. For legitimate technical adjustments, use established image processing software for minimal, uniform edits such as contrast or color correction and refer to Wiley’s Guidelines for the preparation of figures . Use in research methods AI Technologies may be used to analyze photographs as part of your research methodology. These uses should be disclosed in your methods section and may also be included in your supplemental materials. Why this boundary matters Research integrity investigations have repeatedly identified image manipulation as a significant concern. Maintaining clear standards for photograph processing helps preserve trust in the scholarly record and ensures your research evidence remains reliable for scientific evaluation. Creating marketing content with AI Can I use AI to create promotional content based on my published research? Yes. AI can be a powerful ally in communicating and sharing your research with broader audiences. Many researchers find AI particularly helpful for translating complex findings into accessible language, creating engaging social media content, developing podcast scripts, or drafting blog posts that reach new audiences. How AI can help with research outreach: Audience adaptation: Transform technical or complex language for different audiences – for example: general public, policymakers, or students Format variety: Create content across multiple channels – social media posts, newsletter content, presentation summaries Time efficiency: Generate first drafts quickly, leaving you more time to refine and personalize Creative angles: Explore different ways to frame your findings or identify compelling hooks for your research story Best practices for using AI with research outreach: Check your rights: Review your publishing agreement to confirm whether you can adapt your work into other formats (such as podcasts, blogs, or social media posts). Some agreements place these uses under subsidiary rights and may require publisher approval Protect your content: Do not upload full articles or large portions of copyrighted text unless your agreement explicitly allows it. Use only AI Technologies that have privacy protections, such as “do not train” settings or enterprise-grade security Review for accuracy and tone: AI-generated drafts may oversimplify, misrepresent findings, or adopt a tone that doesn’t fit your audience or your personal communication style. Always review in full and edit for accuracy, clarity, and alignment with your personal communication style and goals Be transparent when needed: If AI played a role in producing outreach materials, acknowledge its use in line with institutional or professional guidelines Methods and AI use in developing this guidance This guidance and all author FAQs were developed through direct engagement with authors and editors. We conducted in-depth interviews with over 40 authors and editors across various disciplines to understand how they are incorporating AI Technologies into their work. These conversations revealed nuanced insights that shaped our guidance and highlighted key questions that authors have about confidently and safely applying AI Technologies in their manuscript development. Our development process: Interviewed research authors and editors across various disciplines Reviewed and analyzed current industry guidelines and publisher policies Drafted initial best practices reflecting author, editor, and industry insights Refined guidance and best practices through iterative additions from Wiley subject matter experts, including team members from Legal, Research Integrity, Publishing, and Technology Edited document for consistency, tone, and clarity Generative AI use in research analysis and content development Interviews were recorded and transcribed utilizing Copilot in Microsoft Teams (February 2025 – June 2025). Interview notes were entered and tagged manually by the researchers in Miro (June – July 2025) and then sorted using the clustering feature. All interviews were either directly conducted by or reviewed by the primary author of this work to ensure familiarity with the interview content prior to introducing generative AI Technologies for analysis and drafting. The drafting authors directed content development through extensive collaboration with Claude 4 Sonnet (July 2025). All content shared with the AI Technology was de-identified to ensure interviewee confidentiality. Notes and direct quotes were shared with Claude 4 Sonnet one theme at a time, along with prior Wiley AI guidance as context for the desired output format and content structure. The drafting authors provided editorial direction for initial section drafts, which were composed by Claude 4 Sonnet based on interview findings. Through iterative rounds of review and revision, all text content was refined and rewritten by the author to ensure accuracy, appropriate tone, and alignment with Wiley's vision for supporting authors in responsible AI use. The drafting authors used Claude 4 Sonnet and GPT-5 (July – August 2025) for comprehensive document review to identify potential gaps in flow, consistency, and completeness. All AI recommendations were evaluated and selectively incorporated based on the research team's editorial judgment and subject matter expertise. All AI-generated content was verified against original interview data to ensure accuracy and authentic representation of researcher insights. Once the initial draft was completed, Wiley experts from various disciplinary fields reviewed and updated the document. This expert review and revision ensures that all proposed guidance meets legal and integrity standards. The final draft was revised by the author using Claude 4 Sonnet and GPT-5 (August 2025) and then polished by a human copyeditor with expertise in research communication.
